description: Premium Australian design-led floral lifestyle brand. Fashion-editorial photography — the bouquet and the woman are co-heroes.

dimensions.name: lighting
dimensions.description: Soft, even studio lighting with warm undertone. Slight directional quality from one side creating gentle dimension on face and bouquet. No harsh shadows, no flat flash. Warm colour temperature (3800-4500K). The model's skin and the flower colours should both read beautifully. Backdrop should glow warmly, not read as grey.

dimensions.name: composition
dimensions.description: Model and bouquet are CO-HEROES — both must have visual presence. Three-quarter or full-body framing. Model positioned slightly off-centre. Bouquet occupies at least 30% of frame. Clean, uncluttered frame — no competing background elements. Adequate negative space for the warm backdrop to breathe. Shoot for 4:5 (feed) or 3:4 primary crop.

dimensions.name: colour
dimensions.description: Brand palette is monochrome (Noir #000000, Silk White #FFFFFF, Clay #D8CCBE) — the FLOWERS provide all colour. Flower colours must be vivid and true-to-life: saturated pinks, orchid purples, amber oranges, fuchsia, coral. Backdrop reads as warm clay/terracotta neutral. White tissue wrap reads clean, not blue. No colour grading that mutes or desaturates the flowers — their vibrancy IS the brand.

dimensions.name: styling
dimensions.description: Fashion-forward model styling: considered outfit that complements (not competes with) the bouquet. Clothing in brand palette — black, white, cream, or seasonal fashion pieces. Minimal jewellery (gold hoops, thin bracelet). Bouquet wrapped in white tissue with black satin ribbon. Fig & Bloom branded sticker visible where natural. Model not engaging camera — looking away, contemplative, present.

dimensions.name: technical
dimensions.description: Sharp focus on both model and bouquet (both are heroes — neither can be soft). Professional camera quality, f/4-5.6 for adequate depth across both subjects. No AI artefacts on hands, face, fingers, or flower petals. Clean edges on tissue wrap and ribbon. Skin texture natural, not over-smoothed.

dimensions.name: brand_alignment
dimensions.description: Feels like a fashion brand campaign that happens to feature flowers — not a florist who hired a model. The energy is editorial, intentional, refined. The image should feel at home on a fashion brand's Instagram or in a lifestyle magazine editorial. NOT: generic gift-shop photography, stock photo energy, overly sweet/romantic, stiff catalogue posing.

components: SHOT TYPE — e.g. 'Fashion editorial photograph of...'
SUBJECT — The model description AND the specific bouquet/flowers
STYLING — Model outfit, jewellery, hair, and bouquet wrapping details
BACKDROP — Warm clay/neutral studio backdrop description
CAMERA — Angle, format, framing (three-quarter, full-body, close-crop)
LIGHTING — Soft studio with warm undertone, directional quality
DEPTH OF FIELD — f/4-5.6, both model and bouquet sharp
COLOUR — Vivid true-to-life flower colours against neutral backdrop
POSE — Model gaze direction, body angle, how bouquet is held
PACKAGING — White tissue, black satin ribbon, branded sticker
EXCLUSIONS — 'No text, no logos, no watermarks. Model not looking at camera.'
